_comment: Operative AI control register. Each control is ownable, testable and mapped: it names a risk, the objectives it serves, the framework articles it answers, an accountable owner, the evidence it requires, a test with a pass condition, an exception path, and the residual risk that remains. control_register.py checks fail-closed that every field is present, every objective is covered by >=1 approved control, and every framework_mapping element exists in framework_map.FRAMEWORKS. `implements` links to a verified building block where one exists. This register makes the ten control objectives runnable, not just described.

controls.name: AI accountability and ownership register
controls.risk_statement: An AI system runs with no named accountable owner, so no one is answerable for its risks, changes or incidents.

test.name: every_system_has_accountable_owner
test.pass_condition: Every registered AI system resolves to exactly one accountable owner and a RACI entry.

controls.residual_risk: A named owner can still be under-resourced or inattentive; accountability is necessary, not sufficient.

controls.name: Pre-deployment AI risk assessment and tiering
controls.risk_statement: A system is deployed without assessing its risk tier, so controls are neither selected nor sized to its impact.

test.name: no_deployment_without_tier
test.pass_condition: No system reaches production without an assigned risk tier and a completed risk assessment.

controls.residual_risk: Tiering is a judgement; a mis-tiered system under-applies controls until re-assessed.

controls.name: Human approval for high-impact actions
controls.risk_statement: The agent can take or propose an action with financial, safety or operational consequence without sufficient control.

test.name: high_impact_action_denial_test
test.pass_condition: All high-impact actions without an approved authority are denied (fail-closed).

controls.residual_risk: Human approval can still be wrong or inattentive; approval reduces, not eliminates, high-impact error.

controls.name: Data provenance and classification
controls.risk_statement: The system trains on or retrieves from data of unknown origin, quality or sensitivity, propagating bias or leaking protected data.

test.name: all_sources_classified_and_traced
test.pass_condition: Every data source in scope has a classification and a lineage record; unclassified sources are blocked.

controls.residual_risk: Classification can be stale or wrong; lineage covers known sources only.

controls.name: System and model documentation (system card)
controls.risk_statement: The system's purpose, limits and evaluation are undocumented, so reviewers and users cannot judge fitness or disclose limitations.

test.name: system_card_present_and_current
test.pass_condition: A current system card exists stating purpose, data domain, limits, evaluation and caveats.

controls.residual_risk: A system card documents; it does not guarantee the documented limits are respected in use.

controls.name: Fail-closed policy enforcement (PDP/PEP)
controls.risk_statement: Actions are allowed by default, so an unrecognized or unsafe action executes because nothing explicitly blocked it.

test.name: unknown_action_denied
test.pass_condition: An unrecognized action type and any policy-violating action are denied (fail-closed), verified on an adversarial suite.

controls.residual_risk: Simulator-scoped: the benchmark floor does not prove real tool-call interception; metadata-dependent gaps remain (see REMORA-CLAIM-009).

controls.name: Immutable decision and action logging
controls.risk_statement: Decisions and actions are not durably recorded, so no audit trail exists to reconstruct what happened and why.

test.name: decisions_logged_tamper_evident
test.pass_condition: Every decision and action is written to a tamper-evident log; a modified past entry is detected.

controls.residual_risk: Logging records the decision; it does not by itself prevent a bad decision.

controls.name: Runtime monitoring and drift detection
controls.risk_statement: The deployed system degrades or drifts undetected because nothing monitors its behaviour against expectation.

test.name: drift_alert_fires_on_shift
test.pass_condition: An injected distribution/behaviour shift raises an alert within the defined window (anytime-valid where applicable).

controls.residual_risk: Monitoring covers modelled failure modes; novel failures may pass undetected until observed.

controls.name: Bias and fairness assessment
controls.risk_statement: The system produces systematically worse or discriminatory outcomes for some groups, unmeasured.

test.name: subgroup_disparity_within_threshold
test.pass_condition: Measured subgroup disparity is within the documented threshold, or the gap is disclosed and accepted.

controls.residual_risk: Thinnest control in the library: no machine-checked fairness primitive yet exists; assessment is deployment-specific and metric-dependent.

controls.name: Personal-data protection and minimization
controls.risk_statement: The system collects, retains or exposes more personal data than the purpose requires, breaching privacy obligations.

test.name: no_excess_personal_data
test.pass_condition: Personal data in scope is limited to the documented purpose; a DPIA exists for high-risk processing.

controls.residual_risk: Minimization reduces exposure; it does not eliminate re-identification or downstream misuse risk.

controls.name: Uncertainty-based abstention and escalation
controls.risk_statement: The system answers confidently when it should abstain, producing a fluent but unreliable output on a case it cannot handle.

test.name: abstains_below_confidence_threshold
test.pass_condition: Cases below the calibrated confidence threshold abstain and escalate rather than answer.

controls.residual_risk: Coverage guarantees are marginal and assume exchangeable data; per-instance correctness is not guaranteed.

controls.name: Incident response, rollback and fallback
controls.risk_statement: When the system fails or is compromised, there is no rehearsed path to contain, roll back and continue operating safely.

test.name: incident_drill_passes
test.pass_condition: A rehearsed incident drill contains the failure, rolls back, and restores a safe mode within the target window.

controls.residual_risk: Drills cover rehearsed scenarios; a novel incident may exceed the runbook.

controls.name: Vendor fallback and degraded-mode operation
controls.risk_statement: A model or hyperscaler outage or behaviour change halts the function or silently alters its outputs, with no independent fallback.

test.name: degrades_gracefully_on_vendor_loss
test.pass_condition: On simulated primary-vendor loss, the system degrades to an approved secondary or local mode with no irreversible actions, within the recovery target.

controls.residual_risk: Degraded mode reduces function; a local fallback may not match primary quality.

controls.name: Prompt-injection and tool-misuse defense
controls.risk_statement: Adversarial input in retrieved content or user text manipulates the agent into unsafe actions or manufactured confidence.

test.name: injection_does_not_escalate_authority
test.pass_condition: Injected instructions in query or retrieved content cannot raise the action's authority or bypass the refusal gate; verified on an injection suite.

controls.residual_risk: Defenses are best-effort against a moving adversary; the refusal gate is a filter, and the grounded generator is the authoritative overclaim guard, not a hard boundary.
